messages.content: Hi. I am a software developer specializing in Django, but recently I finished courses in React, Redux, modern JavaScript, HTML and CSS. I am educated in Machine Learning, especially learned image compression (LIC). I want to create a resume that will be clear, where I can present my new skills. Generate for me structure, sections and sample texts.

Sure, let's create a comprehensive resume for you using the following structure:

1. Header
- Include your name and contact information (phone number, email, LinkedIn, Github profile) in this section.

2. Summary/Objective
- Briefly describe your expertise, skills, and objective.
Example:
"Experienced Django developer with knowledge in React, Redux and modern JavaScript. Specialized in implementing Machine Learning techniques, specifically image compression algorithms. Seeking a challenging role to apply my skills and expand my knowledge in developing innovative technologies."

3. Technical Skills
- List your technical skills in bullet points. Example:
Technical Skills:
- Python (Django)
- ReactJS, Redux
- JavaScript (ES6, NodeJS)
- HTML, CSS
- TensorFlow, Keras
- Image compression (LIC)

4. Work Experience
- Describe your work experience, including any relevant projects you worked on, your role, and your achievements.
Example:
Work Experience:
Software Developer - XYZ Company
Full-time, September 2018 - Present
- Developed a web-based platform for social media management using Django, React, and Redux.
- Worked with Machine Learning Specialists to implement image compression algorithms using TensorFlow and Keras.
- Successfully reduced image size by 70%, resulting in faster loading times and reduced server costs.
- Collaborated with the design team to create user-friendly interfaces using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

5. Education
- List your educational background and any relevant courses or certifications. Example:
Education:
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science - AB University
Certifications:
- Machine Learning - Stanford University
- Full-Stack Web Development - Udemy

6. Projects
- This section is optional but can be used to showcase some of the personal projects you have completed and your involvement in open source projects. Remember to include project descriptions, your skills utilized, and any necessary links to Github or online portfolios.

7. References
- This section is also optional but can be integrated if needed. It is typically best to not list references in your resume, but rather have them available upon request.

Great! Let's fill in the sample text for each section:

1. Header
John Doe
123-456-7890
johndoe@email.com
LinkedIn: <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_URL>
Github: www.github.com/johndoe

2. Summary/Objective
I am an experienced Django developer with knowledge in React, Redux and modern JavaScript. I specialize in implementing Machine Learning techniques, specifically image compression algorithms. I am seeking a challenging role to apply my skills and expand my knowledge in developing innovative technologies.

3. Technical Skills
- Python (Django)
- ReactJS, Redux
- JavaScript (ES6, NodeJS)
- HTML, CSS
- TensorFlow, Keras
- Image compression (LIC)

4. Work Experience
Software Developer - XYZ Company
Full-time, September 2018 - Present
- Developed a web-based platform for social media management using Django, React, and Redux.
- Worked with Machine Learning Specialists to implement image compression algorithms using TensorFlow and Keras.
- Successfully reduced image size by 70%, resulting in faster loading times and reduced server costs.
- Collaborated with the design team to create user-friendly interfaces using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

Junior Software Developer - ABC Company
Internship, May 2018 - August 2018
- Assisted in the development of a web-based e-commerce platform using Django and JavaScript.
- Worked with the team to implement responsive design using HTML and CSS.
- Participated in code reviews and bug fixes.

5. Education
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science - AB University
Certifications:
- Machine Learning - Stanford University
- Full-Stack Web Development - Udemy

6. Projects
- Implemented a sentiment analysis model using Python and TensorFlow, achieving an accuracy rate of 85%.
- Developed a personal finance web application using React and Redux, allowing users to track expenses and income.
- Contributed to the open source project "Django-Rest-Framework" on Github, adding new features and improving documentation.

7. References
Available upon request.
Can you list me 10 most common mistakes in developers' resume that are made? Take into account modern approach in resume design.

Sure, here are 10 common mistakes that developers make in their resume:

1. Not tailoring the resume to the job position - It is important to customize your resume to the job description to highlight the relevant skills and experience.

2. Including irrelevant information - Avoid adding information that is not relevant to the job or your skills, such as personal hobbies or unrelated job experience.

3. Using a generic resume template - Using a generic resume template can make you blend in with the crowd, and it won't stand out to potential employers.

4. Making the resume too long - Keep your resume concise and to the point, typically no more than two pages.

5. Grammatical errors and typos - Spelling mistakes and grammatical errors can make a bad first impression and show a lack of attention to detail.

6. Not highlighting your achievements - Be sure to highlight your achievements, such as successful projects or awards, to show your value.

7. Not using bullet points - Bullet points make it easier to read and scan through the resume, allowing the employer to quickly identify your skills and experience.

8. Poor formatting and design - A poorly designed resume can make you appear unprofessional. Use a clean and professional design, with appropriate font sizes and margins.

9. Focusing too much on technical skills - While technical skills are important, employers also want to see soft skills such as communication, collaboration, and problem-solving.

10. Not including a summary or objective - A summary or objective at the beginning of your resume can give the employer an idea of your skills and experience, and why you are a good fit for the job.
Is it a good idea to use progress bars in technical skills? Should I add interests & hobbies section?

Including progress bars in technical skills can be helpful in visually indicating your level of proficiency in each skill. However, it's important to keep in mind that progress bars can be subjective and may not accurately reflect your skills. It's best to use them sparingly and only if you are confident in your proficiency level.

As for adding an interests and hobbies section, it's generally not necessary or recommended unless your hobbies are directly related to the job or showcase a unique skill set. If you do decide to include this section, keep it brief and focused on relevant interests. Remember, the main focus of your resume should be your technical skills and relevant work experience.
Thank you very much. Do you have more tips for me that I didn't think about, but are important?

Sure, here are some additional tips for creating a strong developer resume:

1. Use strong action verbs to describe your experience and achievements. This can make your resume more dynamic and engaging.

2. Quantify your achievements wherever possible. Use numbers or percentages to show the impact of your work, such as the number of users your application served or the percentage of server costs saved.

3. Highlight your teamwork and collaboration skills. Developers often work in teams, so it's important to show that you can work well with others and communicate effectively.

4. Don't include personal information such as your age, marital status, or gender. This information is not relevant to your skills and experience and could lead to unconscious biases.

5. Use a professional email address. Avoid using email addresses that are unprofessional or juvenile, such as "crazycatlady@email.com".

6. Use a modern and clean design. A cluttered or outdated resume can make you appear unprofessional. Use a clean and modern design that is easy to read and understand.

7. Keep your resume up-to-date. As you gain new skills and experience, be sure to update your resume accordingly. This way, you'll be ready to apply for new job opportunities whenever they arise.

8. Proofread and edit your resume multiple times. Your resume is often the first impression that potential employers will have of you, so it's important to make sure it is error-free and well-written.
If I want to get rid of progress bars in resume, how should I replace it?

If you don't want to use progress bars in your resume, you can replace them with bullet points or a simple list of your technical skills. Here's an example:

Technical Skills
- Python (Django)
- ReactJS, Redux
- JavaScript (ES6, NodeJS)
- HTML, CSS
- TensorFlow, Keras
- Image compression (LIC)

Alternatively, you can group your skills into categories, such as "Programming Languages", "Frameworks and Libraries", and "Machine Learning", and list your skills under each category.

Programming Languages:
- Python
- JavaScript

Frameworks and Libraries:
- Django
- ReactJS
- Redux

Machine Learning:
- TensorFlow
- Keras
- Image compression (LIC)

Remember, the most important thing is to make your technical skills easily visible and understandable to potential employers.
Is it better to include a photo in resume or not?

It's generally not recommended to include a photo in your resume, as it can lead to unconscious biases during the hiring process. Additionally, in some countries, it is illegal to ask for a photo during the hiring process to avoid discrimination based on appearance. 

Your skills and experience are what matter most in the hiring process, so it's best to keep the focus on those. By excluding a photo, you can ensure that your potential employer is evaluating you based on your skills and qualifications, rather than your appearance.
Looking at my questions tell me more hints to make my resume perfect and avoid bad, outdated practices.

Certainly! Here are some additional hints to make your resume stand out and avoid outdated practices:

1. Use keywords from the job description in your resume. Many companies use applicant tracking systems (ATS) to screen resumes, so it's important to include relevant keywords in your resume to ensure that it gets past the initial screening process.

2. Use a clear and easy-to-read font. Stick to standard fonts like Arial, Calibri or Times New Roman, and use a font size between 10-12pt. Avoid using fancy or decorative fonts that can make your resume difficult to read.

3. Use bullet points to make your experience and achievements stand out. Bullet points make your resume easier to read and allow the employer to quickly scan through your skills and experience.

4. Don't use outdated or irrelevant information. Leave out information such as your high school education or outdated software or technology skills. Focus on the most recent and relevant skills and experience.

5. Use industry-standard terminology to describe your skills and experience. Avoid using slang or jargon that may not be familiar to employers in your industry.

6. Keep your resume concise and to the point. Aim for a one- or two-page resume and avoid including unnecessary information that doesn't add value.

7. Use active verbs to describe your experience and achievements. Active verbs such as "developed", "created", and "implemented" make your resume more dynamic and engaging.

8. Make your resume visually appealing. Use a modern and clean design with appropriate white space, and include headings and subheadings to break up the text and make it easier to read.

By following these hints, you can create a modern and effective resume that showcases your skills and experience to potential employers.
How can I focus attention visually on the most important parts in my resume?

To visually focus attention on the most important parts of your resume, you can use design elements such as bold text, italics, and headings.

Here are some tips for using design to highlight important parts of your resume:

1. Use bold text to make important keywords and phrases stand out. For example, you might bold the job title and company name for each position you've held.

2. Use italics to emphasize text that you want to draw attention to, such as a certification or award.

3. Use headings and subheadings to break up the text and make it easier to scan. Use a larger font size for headings to make them stand out.

4. Use bullet points to create lists of your experience and achievements. This helps to make the information more readable and scannable.

5. Use white space to create a clean and uncluttered design. Too much text or too many design elements can make your resume difficult to read.

6. Use color sparingly to draw attention to important elements. For example, you might use a color to highlight your name and contact information at the top of your resume.

Remember, the goal is to create a visually appealing and easy-to-read resume that highlights your skills and experience. Use design elements strategically to draw attention to the most important parts of your resume and make it stand out to potential employers.
Do you have somthing to add? If not, then thank you very much for your insight :)

You're welcome! Here are a few more tips to consider:

1. Consider using a summary or profile section at the top of your resume. This can be a great way to showcase your skills, experience, and career goals in a few sentences.

2. Be sure to include any relevant certifications or training courses you've completed. This can help to demonstrate your expertise in specific areas and make you stand out to potential employers.

3. Use numbers and statistics to quantify your achievements wherever possible. This can help to provide context and show the impact of your work.

4. Be consistent in your formatting and design choices. This can help to create a cohesive and professional-looking resume.

5. Always proofread your resume carefully before submitting it. Typos and grammatical errors can be a major turnoff to potential employers.

Best of luck with your resume!
